incidents.summary: The first cases of H5N1 bird flu have been detected in Australian seabirds. A confirmed case was found in a brown skua in Cape Le Grand, Western Australia, and a suspected case in a giant petrel near Hawks Nest, New South Wales. Additionally, CSIRO lab testing confirmed the strain in a greater crested tern.

logs.summary: On November 19, 2025, Mount Semeru in Indonesia erupted, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the sky and threatening air traffic. The eruption prompted the rescue of over 170 stranded climbers, with authorities evacuating more than 900 people in total.

news.headline: Indonesia evacuates stranded climbers after Semeru volcano erupts | Honolulu Star-Advertiser
news.body: JAKARTA >> Indonesian authorities evacuated more than 900 people and were facilitating the safe return on Thursday of 170 climbers stranded after the eruption of its Semeru volcano, one of the country’s tallest mountains.

news.body: Indonesia’s Mount Semeru volcano erupted around 4:00 p.m. local time, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the air, according to data from the country’s Center for Volcanology.

incidents.summary: Severe drought and heat across Europe are shrinking harvests, notably French maize, and causing water shortages in the Netherlands. incidents.summary: A powerful earthquake with a magnitude between 7.4 and 7.7 struck off the northeast coast of Japan on April 20, 2026, prompting a short-lived tsunami warning for coastal areas. The Japan Meteorological Agency issued alerts, and local residents were advised to evacuate to higher ground. Additionally, misinformation about an "artificial earthquake" began spreading online following the event.

incidents.summary: Wildfires continue to rage in Portugal, Spain, and Greece following a June heatwave that caused thousands of excess deaths. logs.summary: A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain's Almería province, starting around July 9-10, 2026, killed at least 13 people and left over 20 missing, many of them foreign nationals (including British and Belgian). The fire, which prompted evacuations of roughly 800 people, is suspected to have been ignited by a broken power line amid a severe summer heatwave. It is considered one of Spain's deadliest wildfires on record.

logs.summary: Spanish authorities identified all 13 victims of the wildfire, confirming 12 were foreign nationals: seven from the UK, three from Belgium, and one each from France and the US. This identification occurred five days after the fire ravaged a remote expat community in Almería.

logs.summary: New details emerged about the Almería wildfire, with reports indicating that victims were killed while attempting to escape along a river bed. The fire has scorched approximately 66 square kilometers (25 square miles) of forest and farmland, with hundreds of firefighters continuing to battle the blaze. Additionally, discussions have begun about implementing bushfire bunkers as a safety measure in Spain.

logs.summary: New details emerged that the victims of the July 9 wildfire in Almería were killed while trying to escape along a river bed. Additionally, authorities are considering the adoption of bushfire bunkers, similar to those used in Australia, as a potential life-saving measure in future wildfires.
